title: Trachelospermum inflatum
intro: Trachelospermum inflatum is a type of climbing woody vine, native to China, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. Bioactive constituents of Trachelospermum inflatum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems.

The species is also classified within the genus Trachelospermum. The genus Trachelospermum is a group of evergreen climbing plants native to Asia.
